name: Pad Prik King
intro: This dish combines the special flavors of Thailand into a quick and easy stir-fry. Crispy chicken and long beans dipped in a sweet salty red curry paste and served with a creamy salted duck egg. Try a vegan version by using fried tofu!
method: Add 2 tablespoons of fish sauce and 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a bowl. Place the chicken in the bowl and toss to coat. Let the chicken marinate for about 15 minutes while you prepare the other ingredients.
Cut the salted duck eggs from the shell and scoop them out in half with a spoon. You cut each half into slices

In a large skillet or wok over high heat, add a generous amount of vegetable oil (enough to coat the bottom of the wok), then fry the chickens for about 1 minute. When done, remove from the pan. The chicken should not be fully cooked at this Step. Also, try not to overcrowd the pan; work in 2 batches if needed.

Turn off the heat and allow the wok to cool slightly, then add about 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil and the curry paste. Stir well, then turn on the heat to medium. Cook the curry paste until it thickens, stirring constantly.
Add palm sugar, 1 teaspoon of fish sauce, and ¼ cup chicken broth. Keep stirring until the sugar is mostly dissolved. If it starts to stick to the pan, add more stock as needed.
Add the shredded kaffir lime leaves into the wok and let it cook for about 30 seconds.
Add the half-cooked chicken (Step 3) into the wok and toss to coat with the sauce.

Add the long beans and cook until the chicken is fully cooked, the sauce is no longer sticky and the curry has coated the chicken and beans. You may also add salted duck eggs (Step 2) at this Step. Stir well.
Remove from heat and transfer to a plate. Garnish with finely sliced kaffir lime leaves.
